title: add newline to images output
body:
ensure a final newline is always added to images output.

fixes #2388

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago ptal
Per @rhatdan this was intentionally omitted when the output was to something not a TTY. Not sure why, though?
yeah lets see what he wants to do with it
@baude in the default `podman images` case, this now emits an unnecessary final newline; probably the if-tty check that @rhatdan mentioned.

Is Mine better?
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2391
@rhatdan I'm rather fond of the simplicity, consistency, and `if`-less-ness of @baude's approach. I don't understand why there has to be any tty check at all.
I think we need to nuke the TTY-specific handling as well here

@edsantiago Does @baude output on a tty and output to | cat match.
My first pass at this was to add an extra blank line when I did not use |cat.
@rhatdan with my review correction (remove `formats.go:123-126`), output is correct for tty and non-tty cases.
/lgtm
@rhatdan can you please un-lgtm until @baude fixes my review comments?
/hold
/lgtm cancel
Should we go back to my PR?

I'd rather not, because yours includes what I think are unnecessary and confusing conditionals for tty.

This is a really, really, super simple fix. @baude, when you have a moment, could you please remove lines 123-126 of formats.go and push?
Yes that looks like the correct answer.
@baude if it helps I submitted a PR against your issue2388 PR
/lgtm
/cancel hold
/hold cancel
